features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported Q3 2022 worldwide sales of $23.8 billion, up 1.9% reported and 8.1% operationally. Adjusted diluted EPS was $2.55, down 1.9% but up 5.8% operationally. All three segments showed operational growth acceleration: Pharmaceuticals grew 9% operationally driven by DARZALEX, TREMFYA, STELARA, and ERLEADA; MedTech grew 8.1% operationally with procedure recovery and new product launches; Consumer Health grew 4.7% operationally despite supply constraints. The company reaffirmed 2022 guidance with operational sales growth of 7% at midpoint and adjusted EPS of $10.73 operationally.
features.management_outlook: Management remains confident in achieving $60 billion pharmaceutical sales by 2025 through organic growth, expecting continued above-market growth despite STELARA loss of exclusivity in late 2023. For 2023, they anticipate positive procedure trends in MedTech, benefits from strategic price increases in Consumer Health, and continued pharmaceutical portfolio strength. The Kenvue Consumer Health separation remains on track for mid-to-late 2023. Management expects inflationary pressures to persist into 2023 but is taking actions to offset these challenges while maintaining R&D investment priorities.
features.risks: Key risks include persistent inflationary pressures across input costs, unfavorable currency translation impacts (estimated $0.40-$0.45 EPS headwind for 2023), STELARA biosimilar competition beginning late 2023, ongoing supply chain constraints, hospital staffing shortages affecting procedures, and volume-based procurement pressures in China. COVID-19 remains a dynamic regional factor affecting procedure volumes. The Consumer Health separation involves execution risks around stranded costs and organizational restructuring for a two-segment company structure.
features.key_drivers: Growth drivers include strong pharmaceutical portfolio performance with DARZALEX (+38.7%), TREMFYA (+41.9%), and ERLEADA (+51.2%) showing robust double-digit growth. MedTech benefits from procedure recovery, new product launches including TECNIS Symfony OptiBlue and ACUVUE OASYS MAX, and strong electrophysiology performance (+19%). Consumer Health is benefiting from strategic price increases and easing supply constraints. The company maintains strong cash generation ($13+ billion free cash flow year-to-date) enabling continued R&D investment (+8% year-to-date) and capital returns ($11 billion to shareholders in 2022).
